content.problemStatement:
If you wanna party, if you, if you wanna party 

Then put your hands up

Chef wants to host a party with a total of NNN people.

However, the party hall has a capacity of XXX people. Find whether Chef can host the party.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and XXX  — the total number of people and the capacity of the party hall.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `YES`, if Chef can host the party and `NO` otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase. That is, the strings `NO`, `no`, `nO`, and `No` will be treated as equivalent.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef wants to host a party with 222 people. Since the capacity of the hall is 555, he can host the party.
Test case 222: Chef wants to host a party with 444 people. Since the capacity of the hall is 333, he can not host the party.
Test case 333: Chef wants to host a party with 666 people. Since the capacity of the hall is 666, he can host the party.
Test case 444: Chef wants to host a party with 101010 people. Since the capacity of the hall is 999, he can not host the party.
